title: Quartz vs. Automatic Watches: Which Should You Actually Buy?
excerpt: The mechanism inside your watch matters more for maintenance and accuracy than most buyers realize.
author: StoreForLess Team
body:
**Quartz watches are more accurate, not less premium**

A common misconception is that quartz movements are lower quality than automatic ones. In reality, quartz watches are significantly more accurate — typically within seconds per month, versus several seconds per day for automatics. Quartz is a battery-powered mechanism, not a lesser one.

**Automatic watches need regular wear to stay accurate**

Automatic watches are powered by wrist movement. If you don't wear one for a few days, it stops and needs to be reset. If you rotate between multiple watches, this is worth considering before buying an automatic.

**Maintenance costs differ a lot**

Quartz watches need a battery replacement every 1-3 years, which is inexpensive. Automatic watches need servicing every 3-5 years, which can cost significantly more depending on the movement.

**What actually affects durability**

Case material and water resistance rating matter more for everyday durability than the movement type. Stainless steel or titanium cases with at least 50m water resistance handle daily wear, splashes, and swimming better than lower-rated alternatives.

**For jewelry pieces, check the plating type**

For rings, bracelets, and necklaces, gold-plated and gold-filled are not the same thing. Gold-filled has a much thicker layer of gold bonded to the base metal and lasts significantly longer before wearing through.
